TextBlock4
childElements.propertySetConfig:
{ "controlWidth" : 12.0, "label" : "TextBlock4", "text" : "

Your application has been saved but will not be considered complete until the application fee has been submitted.

\n

Please click the payment button below to be redirected to the payment page.

\n
    \n
  • The application fee is $25 if payment is made 90 days or more before your event.
    • \n
  • The fee increases to $100 if payment is made less than 90 days before your event.
    • \n
\n

Once your payment has been processed, the Special Events team will notify you regarding the progress of your application.

", "show" : null, "dataJSON" : false, "HTMLTemplateId" : "", "textKey" : "", "sanitize" : false }

childElements.propertySetConfig:

Please upload the documents listed below:

\n
    %DynamicFileText%
\n

All documents are required prior to the issuance of any permits.

\n

The system accepts files in the following formats: gif, jpg, jpeg, png, tiff, bmp, pdf, txt, rtf, csv, doc, docx, xls, xlsx, ppt, pptx, zip.

\n

Each file must not exceed a maximum size of 8 MB.

\n

For assistance completing the Event Site Map please review the Special Events \"Special Event Requirements\" page.

\n

", "show" : null, "dataJSON" : false, "HTMLTemplateId" : "", "textKey" : "", "sanitize" : false }

  • An emergency plan must be uploaded by the organizer in the Document Upload section.  

  • All events will be reviewed by the Fire Department and the Organizer may be contacted for questions/discussion to satisfy Fire Department concerns before the event is approved by the Fire Department.  

  • Any event with over 500 people requires security from a company licensed in the City of St. Louis.   

  • Any event with over 5000 estimated attendees must have Fire Department collaboration in planning.  

  • The organizer must have onsite dedicated professional first aid personnel with a supervisor. 

", "label" : "TextBlock35", "controlWidth" : 12 }

childElements.name: TextBlock5
childElements.propertySetConfig:
{ "controlWidth" : 12, "label" : "TextBlock5", "text" : "

OBTAINING A SPECIAL EVENT PERMIT

We are thrilled that you have chosen the City of St. Louis as the site for your event. To set you up for success there are several permits and reviews you will need so we can ensure your event is properly permitted. Depending on the complexity of your event you may be required to obtain additional permits necessary for things like food, liquor, and street closures.

WHAT EVENTS REQUIRE A SPECIAL EVENT PERMIT?

Events that are open to the public, utilize any part of public property (streets and parks) AND HAVE ONE OR MORE of the following components must complete the special event permitting process: food, liquor, street closures, tents, staging, or generators.

TIME FRAME

For your special event permit applications to be considered, applications must be submitted NO LATER THAN 30 DAYS IN ADVANCE of your event.

  • Special Event Application Non-Refundable Fees are as follows: 
    • $25 - if the application is submitted 90 days or more before the event
    • $100 - if the application is submitted less than 90 days before the event

WHAT EVENTS DO NOT REQUIRE A SPECIAL EVENT PERMIT?

The following types of events do not require a special event permit: 

  • Events located entirely on private property.
  • Residential block parties - a residential, neighbors-only, social gathering, not affiliated with any special event or commercial establishment.
  • Private events (weddings, family reunions, etc.) in a City park.

For residential block parties, please contact the Street Department, and for private events in a City park, please contact the Parks Division.

WHAT TO EXPECT

  1. Complete and submit your special event application no less than 30 days before your event. You will receive a confirmation email and reference number.
  2. Your application will begin processing on the next business day.
  3. The Office of Special Events will then review your application.
  4. If the application is complete, the Office of Special Events will assign your application to any additional departments to also approve.  
  5. The appropriate departments review the application. 
  6. You will be contacted if additional information or clarification of your application is needed. 
  7.  You will be able to check the application status, missing items, and department communication through the OneStopGov at any time.
  8. The Board of Public Service approves your Special Event Permit, once you receive your Special Event Permit you will be prompted to pay for any additional permits (e.g. Streets Permit, Parks Permit) via the OneStopGov. 
  9. Note: The Special Event Permit must be approved before any other permits are obtained.

For additional guidance on event policies and procedures, please review the following documents from the City of St. Louis, Department of Parks, Recreation & Forestry: Ticketed Event Policy in St. Louis City Parks , Special Event Procedures

childElements.name: TentErrorMsg
TextBlock19
childElements.propertySetConfig:
{ "controlWidth" : 12, "label" : "TextBlock18", "text" : "

Because of the event type you have chosen, we need additional information below.

Important Equipment Rental Guidelines

The contact person will be required to meet the crew for the placement of equipment. If we are unable to reach the contact person for placement, the equipment will not be delivered.

PLEASE NOTE:

*The Large Bandwagon/Stage must be on pavement. A generator is not included with the Bandwagon.
*Tents must be placed on grass so they can be staked. Tents cannot be placed on pavement.
*Equipment is not scheduled until rental fees are paid and a confirmation is received from the Park Division regarding item availability.
*The Parks Division reserves the right to adjust the number of items requested based on availability and demand. The contact person will be notified if adjustments are made.
*Any person or organization renting City equipment will be held responsible for repairing or replacing damaged and/or stolen equipment from the date it is delivered to the date it is picked up.

", "show" : null, "dataJSON" : false, "HTMLTemplateId" : "", "textKey" : "", "sanitize" : false }
